worth_it_explanation: On-target percentage (OTP) is the primary signal Nielsen Digital Ad Ratings (DAR) produces for demographic delivery. A campaign can hit its impression goal and still fail to reach its intended audience if OTP is low, never checked in-flight, or read without the measured base that gives the number meaning. These checks surface the three most actionable problems: a shortfall against the media plan goal that goes uninvestigated, an OTP that is reviewed only after the flight has ended, and an OTP figure that is trusted without confirming the measured rate that underlies it. Each check leads to a configuration or workflow change, not a rebuild.
intro: Nielsen DAR measures deduplicated unique audience, reach, frequency, gross rating points (GRPs), and on-target percentage across desktop, mobile, and connected TV (CTV). On-target percentage is the share of total impressions that reached the campaign's intended demographic, calculated by dividing impressions seen by the target demographic by the total impression count for the line item. DAR derives this figure by combining Nielsen's panel data with aggregated census data, producing a demographic estimate that can be compared against the media plan's delivery guarantee. These checks confirm that OTP is held against a documented goal, reviewed at a consistent cadence during the flight, and read alongside the measured rate that determines how reliable the figure is.

audit_checks.title: On-target percentage falls below the media plan goal or guarantee and the shortfall is not documented or addressed
audit_checks.impact_rationale: A campaign that delivers impressions outside its intended demographic is effectively running against the wrong audience. If that shortfall is not caught and documented, the advertiser pays for delivery that did not meet the demographic goal, with no record of the gap and no basis for a make-good or renegotiation.

educate.base:
Nielsen Digital Ad Ratings (DAR) calculates on-target percentage (OTP) as the share of total impressions that reached the campaign's intended demographic. The formula is: impressions seen by the target demographic divided by total impression count for the line item, multiplied by 100. A media plan typically specifies a target demographic, such as adults 25 to 54, and often includes a negotiated OTP guarantee or benchmark the buy is expected to meet.

When the DAR report shows an OTP below the plan goal, that shortfall represents impression delivery outside the intended demographic. The cause can be a targeting misconfiguration in the buying platform, a mismatch between the demographic the deal was negotiated against and the demographic entered in the DAR measurement setup, or genuine audience inventory constraints on a given publisher or supply path. None of these causes is detectable without comparing the reported OTP against the documented goal.

If no plan goal was documented, there is no baseline to compare against, and the OTP figure has no actionable meaning. You may want to confirm that every campaign with DAR measurement active has a documented OTP benchmark before the first impression is delivered, drawn either from the media plan's negotiated guarantee or from Nielsen's published benchmarks for the demographic and format.

A few signs this check applies:
  • The DAR report shows an OTP below 50% for a demographic-targeted campaign with no explanation on file for the shortfall
  • The media plan or insertion order (IO) specifies an OTP guarantee but no post-flight review compares the delivered OTP against that figure
  • The campaign's targeting setup in the buying platform uses a demographic that does not match the demographic group entered in the DAR measurement configuration
You may want to confirm the OTP benchmark is documented and that any shortfall against it is reviewed and noted before the campaign closes.

site_type_overlays.ecommerce: For a retailer running a display or video campaign targeted to adults 18 to 49, a DAR OTP shortfall means impressions reached a demographic outside the purchase-intent window the media plan was designed around. You may want to confirm the OTP benchmark reflects the demographic the creative and offer were built for, so a shortfall triggers a review rather than going unnoticed until post-campaign.
site_type_overlays.lead_gen: For a lead-generation advertiser targeting a specific professional demographic, OTP is particularly relevant because the conversion pipeline depends on reaching people with decision-making authority. You may want to confirm the OTP goal is documented and that the demographic entered in the DAR setup matches the audience the IO describes.

learn_more.source_title: Metrics for Nielsen Digital Ad Ratings: On Target Percent definition
Measure demographic targeting success with Nielsen Digital Ad Ratings

investigate.intro: Pull the DAR report for the campaign and compare the reported OTP against the media plan's documented goal or guarantee. Confirm the demographic in the DAR setup matches the demographic on the IO.

nielsen_dar.label: Nielsen DAR
steps: Open the DAR report for the campaign. Locate the 'On Target Percent (%)' column, which reports impressions delivered to the target demographic divided by total impressions for the line item.
Compare the reported OTP against the media plan's documented OTP goal or the IO's negotiated guarantee. Note the absolute percentage point gap.
Check the demographic group configured in the DAR measurement setup (e.g., Adults 25-54). Confirm it matches the demographic specified in the IO.

If the OTP is below the goal, review the buying platform's targeting settings for the affected line items to identify whether the demographic targeting entered there aligns with the DAR measurement demographic.
Note whether any explanation for the shortfall has been documented. If none exists, record the shortfall amount and the demographic discrepancy, if any, as a finding.

evaluate.logic: The practitioner records the reported OTP and the documented plan goal or guarantee for the campaign. If OTP meets or exceeds the documented goal and the demographic setup matches the IO, the check passes. If OTP falls below the documented goal, or if no goal was documented, or if the demographic in the DAR setup does not match the IO, the check is a fail, recorded with the reported OTP, the goal, and the nature of the discrepancy.
evaluate.fallback_message: Record the reported on-target percentage and the documented plan goal or IO guarantee above to see the evaluation.

generate.narrative_template: {{voice_found}} that {{site_name}}'s campaign on-target percentage as reported by Nielsen Digital Ad Ratings fell below the media plan goal, with no documentation of the shortfall or investigation into its cause. When impressions deliver outside the intended demographic and that gap goes unaddressed, the advertiser pays for delivery that did not meet the negotiated demographic standard. {{voice_recommend}} documenting an on-target percentage benchmark before each campaign begins, comparing the reported OTP against that benchmark at the campaign's close, and reviewing the demographic configuration in the buying platform and the DAR setup when a shortfall exceeds a few percentage points.

audit_checks.title: On-target performance is reviewed only after the flight ends, with no in-flight checkpoint to catch and correct a low-performing delivery
audit_checks.impact_rationale: DAR data becomes available within roughly three days of delivery. A campaign that is running with low OTP can be identified and corrected mid-flight, but only if someone reviews the data while the campaign is still active. Reviewing OTP only at the end of a flight means any demographic delivery problem ran for the full duration with no opportunity to intervene.

educate.base:
Nielsen Digital Ad Ratings (DAR) data is not available in real time. Impression data from the ad server reaches Nielsen's systems and is processed into demographic measurement reports with a lag, typically around three days. Buying platforms that integrate DAR, including Google Ad Manager, perform nightly adjustments based on in-target delivery data once it arrives. This means in-flight correction is possible, but only if the practitioner reviews the data while the campaign is running and has a process for acting on what it shows.

A campaign that launches with a demographic targeting configuration problem, or that is running against supply with poor audience coverage for the target demographic, will produce a low OTP reading within the first few days of delivery. If no one reviews the DAR data until the campaign ends, that low OTP ran for the entire flight. The impression budget delivered outside the target demographic cannot be recovered, and the advertiser receives a post-campaign report that shows poor demographic delivery with no record of any attempt to address it during the flight.

In-flight review does not require complex analysis. It requires checking the OTP column in the DAR report against the documented benchmark at a set interval, typically weekly for campaigns longer than two weeks. If OTP is below the benchmark, the buying platform targeting should be reviewed and, where possible, adjusted before the next delivery cycle.

A few signs this check applies:
  • The team's standard practice is to review DAR reports only when pulling post-campaign performance data
  • No calendar event, workflow step, or report schedule exists for mid-flight DAR review
  • The campaign has run for more than one week with no review of the OTP column in the DAR report
You may want to confirm that a mid-flight review step is built into the campaign workflow before the next flight with DAR measurement begins.

site_type_overlays.ecommerce: For a retailer running a seasonal campaign with a fixed flight window, a low OTP identified in week two of a four-week flight still allows two weeks of corrective adjustment. You may want to confirm a mid-flight review cadence is set at campaign launch so a demographic delivery problem does not consume the full budget before anyone sees it.
site_type_overlays.lead_gen: For a lead-generation advertiser running a fixed-budget flight, OTP directly affects how many of the impressions delivered reached the demographic the offer was designed for. You may want to confirm a weekly DAR review step is in the campaign workflow so a targeting issue can be corrected before a significant share of the budget has been spent.

learn_more.source_title: Nielsen Digital Ad Ratings pacing and reports (Beta)
Nielsen Digital Ad Ratings measurement features

investigate.intro: Confirm whether a mid-flight DAR review cadence exists for the campaign and whether any OTP data was reviewed while the campaign was active.

nielsen_dar.label: Nielsen DAR
steps: Open the DAR report for the campaign and check whether any data was pulled during the flight dates rather than only after the end date. Look for report access timestamps or ask the team when the DAR data was last reviewed during the campaign.
Ask the team whether a scheduled report or calendar reminder exists for mid-flight DAR review. Note whether any such process is documented in the campaign workflow.
If the campaign is still running, pull the current OTP reading and compare it to the documented benchmark. Record the OTP to date and the remaining flight budget.

If the campaign has ended, check whether any campaign notes, emails, or change logs reference a mid-flight OTP review or a targeting adjustment made in response to DAR data. Note the absence of such documentation as a finding.

evaluate.logic: The practitioner records whether evidence exists of a mid-flight DAR review for the campaign. A campaign with a documented weekly or bi-weekly review cadence, or with evidence of a targeting adjustment made in response to in-flight OTP data, passes. A campaign where DAR data was reviewed only at the campaign's end, or where no review cadence existed, is a fail, recorded with the campaign duration and the first date DAR data was accessed.
evaluate.fallback_message: Record whether a mid-flight DAR review cadence exists and the first date DAR data was reviewed for the campaign above to see the evaluation.

generate.narrative_template: {{voice_found}} that {{site_name}}'s campaigns with Nielsen Digital Ad Ratings measurement are reviewed only at the campaign's close, with no in-flight checkpoint to identify and address low on-target delivery while the flight is still active. DAR data becomes available within roughly three days of delivery, which means a demographic targeting problem that surfaces early in a flight can be corrected if someone reviews the data before the budget is spent. {{voice_recommend}} establishing a weekly mid-flight review step for any campaign with DAR measurement active, comparing the current on-target percentage against the documented benchmark, and adjusting buying-platform targeting when a shortfall is identified while budget remains.

audit_checks.title: On-target percentage is reported without the measured rate or audience coverage, making a high OTP on a small measured base appear stronger than it is
audit_checks.impact_rationale: On-target percentage is a ratio. A campaign that delivers 90% OTP on a 10% measured rate has confirmed only that 9% of its impressions reached the target demographic. Reporting OTP in isolation overstates confidence in demographic delivery and can lead to a positive post-campaign narrative that the underlying data does not support.

educate.base:
Nielsen Digital Ad Ratings (DAR) can only classify an impression as in-target or out-of-target if it has data to make a demographic determination for that impression. The share of total impressions for which Nielsen can make that determination is often called the measured rate or coverage rate. Impressions where no demographic determination is possible are excluded from the OTP calculation when no Nielsen panel match or third-party data was available for that device or user.

The measured rate varies across supply sources, device types, and campaign configurations. A campaign running heavily on connected TV (CTV) inventory or across supply paths with limited identity resolution may have a low measured rate, meaning OTP is calculated on a small fraction of total impressions. A 75% OTP figure on a 20% measured rate means Nielsen confirmed demographic delivery on 20% of impressions and found that 75% of those were in-target. The 80% of impressions with no demographic determination are neither counted as in-target nor out-of-target.

Reporting OTP without the measured rate hides this context. A practitioner who sees 75% OTP without seeing that the measured rate was 20% may conclude the campaign delivered well against its demographic, when the evidence covers only a fifth of the campaign's impressions. The audience reach and unique audience count in the DAR report, alongside the total impression count, give a clearer picture of how much of the campaign's delivery has been demographically verified.

A few signs this check applies:
  • Post-campaign DAR reporting highlights OTP but does not include measured impressions, total impressions, or the ratio between them
  • The campaign ran primarily on CTV or programmatic supply paths where identity resolution is lower, and no note exists about the expected measured rate for those channels
  • Unique audience and audience reach figures in the DAR report are low relative to total impressions, suggesting a limited measured base
You may want to confirm that DAR reporting templates include both the OTP and the measured impression count alongside total impressions, so the two figures are read together rather than separately.

site_type_overlays.ecommerce: For a retailer running a CTV campaign where co-viewing and limited device identity resolution can lower the measured rate, you may want to confirm the post-campaign report includes the measured impression count alongside OTP so the demographic delivery conclusion reflects the actual coverage of the measurement.
site_type_overlays.lead_gen: For a lead-generation advertiser running programmatic display across a broad supply mix, the measured rate can vary significantly by supply source. You may want to confirm the DAR report segments OTP by channel or device type so a strong OTP figure on a high-identity-resolution channel is not read as representative of the full campaign.

learn_more.source_title: Metrics for Nielsen Digital Ad Ratings: On Target Percent, Unique Audience, and Audience Reach
Run a Nielsen Digital Ad Ratings report

investigate.intro: Review the DAR report to confirm that OTP is presented alongside the total impression count and the measured impression count, and note the ratio between the two.

nielsen_dar.label: Nielsen DAR
steps: Open the DAR report for the campaign. Locate the 'On Target Percent (%)' column alongside the 'Total Impressions' and 'On Target Impressions' columns.

Calculate the measured rate by dividing the impressions Nielsen classified (in-target plus out-of-target) by the total impressions from the ad server. If the DAR report does not surface this directly, compare the 'Unique Audience' figure against the expected audience size for the campaign's reach goal.
Note whether the standard reporting template includes both OTP and the measured impression count in the same view, or whether OTP is surfaced separately without the coverage context.
If the campaign ran on CTV, check whether the measured rate on CTV placements differs from desktop or mobile placements. DAR's coverage can vary by device type and supply source.

evaluate.logic: The practitioner records the OTP, the total impression count, and the measured impression count from the DAR report. If the standard report template includes all three figures and the measured rate is above a reasonable threshold for the channel mix, the check passes. If OTP is reported without the measured impression count, or if the measured rate is low enough to materially limit confidence in the OTP figure, the check is a fail, recorded with the OTP, the total impression count, and the calculated measured rate.
evaluate.fallback_message: Record the on-target percentage, the total impression count, and the measured impression count from the DAR report above to see the evaluation.

generate.narrative_template: {{voice_found}} that {{site_name}}'s post-campaign reporting highlights Nielsen Digital Ad Ratings on-target percentage without including the measured impression count or the ratio of measured to total impressions. On-target percentage is calculated only on the impressions for which Nielsen could make a demographic determination, so a strong OTP figure on a low measured base covers a fraction of the campaign's actual delivery. {{voice_recommend}} updating the standard DAR reporting template to include total impressions, measured impressions, and the calculated measured rate alongside OTP so the demographic delivery conclusion reflects the coverage of the measurement, not only the ratio within it.
